---
title: "Presentation Title"
subtitle: "Subtitle · Course or event name"
author: "Your Name"
institute: "Western Norway University of Applied Sciences"
date: today
date-format: "D MMMM YYYY"

format:
  revealjs:
    theme: hvl-theme.scss
    slide-number: false
    progress: true
    hash: true
    controls: false
    transition: slide
    transition-speed: fast
    highlight-style: github
    code-line-numbers: true
    embed-resources: false   # set true for a self-contained HTML

title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-color: "#004357"
  data-background-image: "hvl_logo_en_neg.png"
  data-background-size: "180px"
  data-background-position: "top 1.5em right 1.5em"

execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Agenda {.section-slide background-color="#00AFBA"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="48%"}
1. Background & motivation
2. Research questions
3. Methods
:::
::: {.column width="48%"}
4. Key results
5. Discussion
6. Take-aways
:::
:::


## Standard Content Slide

::: {.ingress}
An ingress sentence states the one thing this slide is about.
:::

- First key point with enough detail to be meaningful
- Second point — **bold for emphasis**, *italics for nuance*
- Third point — notice the serif body font echoes the HVL brand
  - Sub-bullet with supporting detail

::: {.fragment}
::: {.fact-box}
**Key takeaway** — use `.fact-box` to surface a single finding at the end of a slide.
:::
:::


## Two-Column: Text + Text

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Left heading

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.

- Point one
- Point two
- Point three
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Right heading

::: {.hvl-accent}
Use `.hvl-accent` for a sea-green left-border accent — good for
definitions, quotes, or key statements.
:::

Additional supporting text.
:::
:::


## Two-Column: Text + Image

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="52%"}
### Finding or argument

Pair a concise argument on the left with a supporting image on the right.

- Relevant point A
- Relevant point B
- Relevant point C
:::
::: {.column width="48%"}
![Replace with your image](https://placehold.co/560x400/C9D5DA/004357?text=Your+image){fig-alt="Placeholder"}
:::
:::


## Three-Column: Statistics

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="40%"}
::: {.vcenter}
[4 600+]{.big-number}
[Students across five campuses]{.big-label}
:::
:::
::: {.column width="35%"}
::: {.vcenter}
[70%]{.big-number}
[Graduate employment rate]{.big-label}
:::
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
::: {.vcenter}
[38]{.big-number}
[Research groups]{.big-label}
:::
:::
:::

::: {.small .muted}
Source: HVL annual report 2024.
:::


## {background-image="https://placehold.co/1280x720/004357/00AFBA?text=Background+image" background-size="cover" background-opacity="0.55"}

::: {.glass-box style="max-width:52%; margin-top:2em;"}
### Big statement over an image

Use `.glass-box` to overlay readable text on a background photo.
The semi-transparent panel keeps text legible on busy images.
:::


## {.dark-slide background-color="#004357"}

::: {.r-fit-text}
"The horizon is wide —

think globally,

act locally."
:::

::: {.small .muted style="text-align:right;"}
— HVL Profilmanual, 2017
:::

## Auto-Animate: Step 1 {auto-animate=true}

Animation

```r
library(ggplot2)
```

## Auto-Animate: Step 2 {auto-animate=true}

```r
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +
  geom_point(color = "#00AFBA", size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Fuel efficiency vs weight",
       x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "Miles per gallon") +
  theme_minimal()
```

Animation

## Code Highlighting

Step through code with `code-line-numbers`.

```{r}
#| code-line-numbers: "1|5|9-10"
library(tidyverse)

# Load and clean
df <- mtcars |>
  mutate(cyl = factor(cyl))

# Summarise
summary_df <- df |>
  group_by(cyl) |>
  summarise(mean_mpg = mean(mpg), .groups = "drop")
```


## Incremental Reveal

::: {.incremental}
- Start with one idea
- Add the next — audience stays focused on what's new
- Then the third point
- Finally the conclusion
:::

The paragraph is already visible. Each click highlights
[the most important phrase]{.fragment .hl-teal},
then [a secondary point]{.fragment .hl-yellow}.

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
::: {.callout-note}
## Note
Fragment highlight classes: `.hl-teal`, `.hl-yellow`.
Text stays visible; only the background animates in on each click.
:::
:::


## Live R Output

```{r}
#| fig-height: 3.2
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("4" = "#00AFBA", "6" = "#008AAF", "8" = "#004357"),
    name = "Cylinders"
  ) +
  labs(x = "Weight (1 000 lbs)", y = "Miles per gallon") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```


## Speaker Notes

Everything here is visible to the audience.

::: {.notes}
This text only appears in speaker view (press **S**).

- Talking points, timing cues, references
- You can use *markdown* in notes
:::


## Summary {.section-slide background-color="#00AFBA"}

- Key finding one — [teal emphasis]{.teal}
- Key finding two
- Key finding three
- Implications for practice / policy / future research


## {.dark-slide background-color="#004357"}

![](hvl_logo_en_neg.png){height="55px"}

### Thank you

::: {.ingress}
Your Name · [your.name@hvl.no](mailto:your.name@hvl.no)
:::

::: {.small .muted}
Department · Western Norway University of Applied Sciences · hvl.no
:::


<!-- ── CHEATSHEET ─────────────────────────────────────────────
SLIDE BACKGROUNDS  (use Quarto attributes, not CSS)
  {background-color="#00AFBA"}          any slide
  {.section-slide background-color="#00AFBA"}   + white text
  {.dark-slide    background-color="#004357"}   + teal/white text
  title-slide-attributes:
    data-background-color: "#004357"    title slide

AUTO-ANIMATE
  ## Slide A {auto-animate=true}
  ## Slide B {auto-animate=true}
  Elements with matching data-id morph between slides:
    ::: {data-id="foo"} ... :::   (div, image wrapper, etc.)
  Without data-id, RevealJS matches by element type + position.

BACKGROUND IMAGE
  ## Heading {background-image="file.jpg" background-size="cover"}

CODE HIGHLIGHTING
  ```{r}
  #| code-line-numbers: "1|2-4|5"

ABSOLUTE POSITIONING  (element written last = on top)
  ::: {.absolute top="20%" right="5%" width="35%"} ... :::

CUSTOM CLASSES
  .ingress          lead sentence in teal DM Sans
  .hvl-accent       sea-green left-border accent bar
  .fact-box         highlighted information box
  .glass-box        dark semi-transparent overlay for images
  .big-number       large teal statistic
  .big-label        small grey label beneath big-number
  .teal .blue .muted  colour helpers
  .vcenter          vertically centre a column
  .small .tiny      font-size helpers

FRAGMENT HIGHLIGHTS  (text visible throughout; bg animates in)
  [phrase]{.fragment .hl-teal}
  [phrase]{.fragment .hl-yellow}
  [phrase]{.fragment .hl-blue}
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
